schema_org.headline: Running AnythingLLM for Free Using Your ChatGPT Plus Account
schema_org.description: Tutorial showing how to configure AnythingLLM with a ChatGPT OAuth token via a local Node.js proxy, removing the need for a paid OpenAI API key.
schema_org.keywords: AnythingLLM, ChatGPT, OAuth, OpenAI, opencode, proxy, Docker, self-hosted AI

geo.summary_one_line: How to run AnythingLLM locally on Docker using a ChatGPT Plus OAuth token through a small Node.js proxy that mimics opencode's request rewriting.
geo.summary_paragraph: AnythingLLM normally requires a paid OpenAI API key. This guide shows how to route its requests through a local proxy on port 11435 that swaps the OpenAI API base URL for chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses — the same endpoint the ChatGPT web app uses. The proxy injects a ChatGPT OAuth JWT (extracted from opencode's auth.json), strips the SDK's auto Authorization header, adds the ChatGPT-Account-Id header, forces streaming, sets store:false, remaps unsupported models to gpt-5.4, and refreshes the token automatically. AnythingLLM is pointed at the proxy via OPENAI_BASE_URL.
prerequisites: opencode CLI installed and authenticated with OpenAI
questions_answered: Can I use my ChatGPT Plus subscription with AnythingLLM instead of paying for an OpenAI API key?
Why does the ChatGPT OAuth token return 401 'Missing scopes: api.responses.write' when used with the OpenAI API?
How does opencode authenticate to OpenAI using a ChatGPT login?
What endpoint does the ChatGPT web app actually call?
How do I redirect AnythingLLM's OpenAI SDK calls to a custom URL?
How do I refresh an expiring ChatGPT OAuth access token?
What is the difference between api.openai.com and chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses?
How do I run AnythingLLM in Docker with persistent storage?
key_facts: AnythingLLM is an open-source local AI chat app from Mintplex Labs.
ChatGPT OAuth access tokens are short-lived JWTs issued by auth.openai.com.
Browser ChatGPT scopes (openid, profile, email, offline_access) do not include api.responses.write.
The ChatGPT web app calls https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses, not api.openai.com.
opencode stores its OpenAI credentials at ~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json.
OPENAI_BASE_URL is the official OpenAI Node SDK environment variable for redirecting requests.
The ChatGPT backend endpoint requires stream:true and store:false on every request.
Models like gpt-4o must be remapped to gpt-5.4 to be accepted by the ChatGPT backend.
Token refresh is performed against https://auth.openai.com/oauth/token using the stored refresh token.
From a Docker container, the host machine is reachable via host.docker.internal.

code_artifacts.name: proxy.js
code_artifacts.purpose: Translates OpenAI API requests to chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses and refreshes tokens.

code_artifacts.name: setup-openai-auth.js
code_artifacts.purpose: Reads opencode's auth.json, verifies token expiry, writes AnythingLLM .env file.

claims_with_sources.claim: opencode rewrites OpenAI SDK requests to chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses via a fetch interceptor.
The OpenAI Node SDK honors OPENAI_BASE_URL for redirecting all API calls.
AnythingLLM's official Docker deployment uses /app/server/storage and a mounted .env file.

geo.novelty: Documents a working pattern — proxying AnythingLLM through the ChatGPT web backend using opencode-style OAuth — that is not described in either project's official docs.
limitations: Relies on the undocumented chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses endpoint; OpenAI may change or rate-limit it at any time.
Subject to ChatGPT Plus usage caps, not API rate limits.
Token refresh assumes opencode's auth.json schema remains stable.
Embeddings fall back to a local native model; OpenAI embeddings are not routed through this proxy.
May violate OpenAI's terms of service for ChatGPT — use at your own risk.

related_concepts.definition: Compact, URL-safe token format used for authentication; the ChatGPT OAuth access token is a JWT.
Permission strings attached to a token that limit which APIs it can call.
Wrapping of the global fetch function to modify requests/responses before they are sent.
OpenAI's streaming, tool-aware API that supersedes Chat Completions for agent workloads.
A server that forwards client requests to one or more upstream servers, optionally transforming them.

glossary.definition: Open-source local AI chat application by Mintplex Labs supporting documents, web search, and agents.
Open-source AI coding CLI by SST that authenticates to OpenAI via the ChatGPT OAuth flow.
Short-lived JWT issued by auth.openai.com for ChatGPT web app sessions.
OAuth scope required for OpenAI's official Responses API; absent from ChatGPT web tokens.
HTTP header identifying which ChatGPT account a backend request belongs to.
Environment variable read by the OpenAI Node SDK to override the API base URL.
Docker Desktop DNS name resolving to the host machine from inside a container.

outline.heading: Why You Can't Just Use the Token Directly

wiki.disclaimer: This setup relies on undocumented ChatGPT backend endpoints and may violate OpenAI's terms of service. Use only on your own ChatGPT account and at your own risk.
